verifier_values.criteria: States that the total number of beds needed is 140
verifier_values.negative_criteria: States that the total number of beds needed is a value other than 140
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
Identify the number of unfit facilities in each state using file "Facility_Requirements_Checklist_v0.3.docx" and page 2.

State	| ✅ Fully Met |	⚠️ Partial	 | ❌ Not Met
Illinois	| 12	| 6	| 2
Indiana	| 8	| 4	| 2
Michigan	| 10	| 4	| 1
Minnesota |	9	| 2	| 0
Missouri|	6	| 3	| 2
Ohio	| 11	| 5	| 1
Wisconsin	| 9	| 2	1

Considering "Partial" & "Not Met" as unfit facilities, the total number of unfit facilities= 35

Number of beds = Number of facilities * 2 * 2 = 140 (every facility has 2 rooms, with 2 beds each as per the prompt), refer to  working file, tab "#beds required" , cell (A1:F9)

verifier_values.criteria: States that the order acceptance rate for "Therapy" beds by Vendor 1 is 33.33% (acceptable range 33.00% to 33.66%)
verifier_values.negative_criteria: States that the order acceptance rate for "Therapy" beds by Vendor 1 is a value other than 33.33%
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
2a. Calculation of total order & rejected order - 
From the raw data, Using Pivot or SUMIFS function (Refer to working file , tab "Acceptance rate calculation" Pivot on cell A1), first we will calculate the total number of orders for each vendor delivered in the months of January, February, March, April, May or June for these 4 years, 2021-2024 (For bed type= "Therapy"). We will also add the split of rejected/ accepted in the same table. We will get the following output

Filters: 
To be considered	1	
Bed Types 	Therapy	

Table		
Row Labels	| Count of Accepted (Y/N)	| Count of Order no 
Vendor1 |	6	| 9
Vendor2 |	5	| 12
Vendor3	| 4	| 9
Grand Total	| 15 | 	30

Total beds required = 140 

2b. calculation of rejection rate and acceptance rate 
From the raw data, Using Pivot mentioned in 2a. (Refer to working file , tab "Acceptance rate calculation" Pivot on cell A1), first we will calculate the rejection rate for each of the vendor by dividing the no of rejected order by no of total orders delivered in the months of January, February, March, April, May or June for these 4 years, 2021-2024 (For bed type= "Therapy").  For calculations refer to working file , tab "Acceptance rate calculation" Table on cell A1:E8. We will get the following output 

Row Labels	| Rejection rate  |	Acceptance Rate 
Vendor1 |	67% |	33.33%
Vendor2	| 42%	| 58.33%
Vendor3 |	44%	| 55.56%
Grand Total	| 50%	| 50.00%

verifier_values.criteria: States that the order acceptance rate for "Therapy" beds by Vendor 2 is 58.33% (acceptable range 57.75% to 58.91%)
verifier_values.negative_criteria: States that the order acceptance rate for "Therapy" beds by Vendor 2 is a value other than 58.33%

verifier_values.criteria: States that average TAT for therapy beds for vendor 1 is 159 days (acceptable range 157 to 161)
verifier_values.negative_criteria: States that average TAT for therapy beds for vendor 1 is a value other than 159 days
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
3a. Average TAT calculation for each vendor -
In the raw data calculate TAT in column I by subtracting the date of order accepted in Column D by date of order delivered in Column E (refer to working sheet, Tab "Data Worked", cell I1). Using pivot , TAT data and SUMIFS, we will calculate the average TAT for each vendor delivered in the months of January, February, March, April, May or June for these 4 years, 2021-2024 (For bed type= "Therapy"). For calculations refer to working file , tab "Average TAT calculation" Table on cell A1:B8. We will get the following output 

Row Labels  |  Average of TAT
Vendor1  |	159.11 - Rounded to 159 days
Vendor2  |	25.7 - Rounded to 26 days
Vendor3  |   165.75  - Rounded to 166 days

verifier_values.criteria: States that the average TAT for therapy beds for vendor 2 is 26 days (acceptable range 25.7 to 26.3)
verifier_values.negative_criteria: States that the average TAT for therapy beds for vendor 2 is a value other than 26 days

Average order value for each vendor = Based on the pricing scheme and total no of beds required (140), first calculate the price for 50 beds at the price per bed for first 50 beds of respective vendors, second calculate the price for 30 beds  at the price per bed for (50-80 beds) of respective vendors, third calculate the  price for 60 beds  at the price per bed for (80-150 beds) of respective vendors (Refer to working file , tab "Avg order value" matrix on cell A1:D18). Vendor 2		9700.00

verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
3.  Determination of vendor's ability to deliver before the task "Room Retrofitting" is concluded

3a. Average TAT calculation for each vendor -
In the raw data calculate TAT in column I by subtracting the date of order accepted in Column D by date of order delivered in Column E (refer to working sheet, Tab "Data Worked", cell I1). Using pivot , TAT data and SUMIFS, we will calculate the average TAT for each vendor delivered in the months of January, February, March, April, May or June for these 4 years, 2021-2024 (For bed type= "Therapy"). For calculations refer to working file , tab "Average TAT calculation" Table on cell A1:B8. We will get the following output 

Row Labels  |  Average of TAT
Vendor1  |	159.11
Vendor2  |	25.7
Vendor3  |   165.75
Grand Total	| 114.55

3b. Task "Room Retrofitting" conclusion date - 
From the timeline referred to in "Phased Rollout Roadmap v0.1.xlsx" ,  "Room Retrofitting"  is completed at the end of week 13,. First we will calculate the total no of days in 13 weeks and then we will add it to the starting date (4 Jan, 2026) and subtract 1 (for existing day - to avoid double counting) to get the final date (Refer to working file , tab "Dates calculation" matrix on cell A1:E3). We will get the following output 

Completed (wk) | 	Days 	| Start date | 	End date 
13	| 91	| 04-Jan-26 |	04-Apr-26

Based on TAT only Vendor 2 will be able to deliver on time


4. Average order value for each vendor = Based on the pricing scheme and total no of beds required (140), first calculate the price for 50 beds at the price per bed for first 50 beds of respective vendors, second calculate the price for 30 beds  at the price per bed for (50-80 beds) of respective vendors, third calculate the  price for 60 beds  at the price per bed for (80-150 beds) of respective vendors (Refer to working file , tab "Avg order value" matrix on cell A1:D18). We will get the following output 

Vendor 1		2750.00
Vendor 2		9700.00
Vendor 3		5950.00

verifier_values.criteria: States that the last acceptable ordering date for the recommended vendor is 1st March, 2026
verifier_values.negative_criteria: States that the last acceptable ordering date for the recommended vendor is a value other than 1st March, 2026
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
5. Last acceptable ordering date = 
5a. Order timeline calculation : Based on last delivery date (4-apr-2026) & the ordering timeline calculated based on average TAT of vendor 2 (26 days) (Refer to working file , tab "Dates Calculation" matrix on cell B21:C26). We will get the following output 

Activity	| #days 
Order placement 	| 1
Vendor acceptance 	| 7
TAT of selected vendor |	26
Ordering timeline  |	34

5b. The last day of ordering is calculated by subtracting the ordering timeline (34 days) form the last acceptable delivery date. Refer to working file , tab "Dates Calculation" matrix on cell A28:B28. We will get the following output 

Last date of ordering	01-03-2026
